description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 40-43.

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Druid table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Cantrips

Prepared

Known

Spells

Legendary Forms

Also at 20th level, you gain the ability to transform into greater forms as part of your wild shape. When you use your Wild Shape, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to turn into any elemental, fey, or monstrosity you have seen before of a Challenge Rating equal to or lower than 10. You transform into an average example of that creature, one without any class levels or the Spellcasting trait. You can’t use any legendary actions, legendary resistances, or lair actions of the new form.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
